name: Patricia Novak
chief_complaint: My left leg has been hurting and swelling up for the past 3 days and today the calf is red and really tender to touch
onset_description: Noticed some left calf soreness 3 days ago, thought she pulled a muscle. Swelling started 2 days ago and has been getting progressively worse. The redness started today. She called her primary care doctor who told her to go to the emergency department immediately.

leg_pain.description: Deep aching pain in my left calf that throbs, really hurts when I walk or flex my foot
leg_pain.severity: 6/10 at rest, 8/10 with walking or dorsiflexion
leg_pain.location: left posterior calf, extending toward the inner knee
leg_pain.radiation: up toward the back of the knee
alleviating_factors: ibuprofen takes the edge off slightly

leg_swelling.description: Left leg is noticeably bigger than the right, from the foot up past the knee now
leg_swelling.character: unilateral swelling, tense, non-pitting becoming pitting
leg_swelling.location: left lower extremity, foot to above knee

erythema_and_warmth.description: The skin over my calf is red and feels hot to the touch, started this morning
erythema_and_warmth.character: diffuse redness over posterior calf, warm

mild_shortness_of_breath.description: I've been a little winded the last day or so, nothing terrible but I noticed it going up the stairs

labs.CBC: WBC 9.4, Hgb 12.8, Plt 310 - within normal limits
labs.BMP: Na 139, K 4.1, Cr 0.8, Glucose 110 - BUN 15 - all within normal limits
labs.liver_panel: AST 24, ALT 28, Alk Phos 78 - within normal limits
labs.duplex_ultrasound: Non-compressible left common femoral vein extending to popliteal vein. Acute thrombus visualized. No flow detected in affected segments. Consistent with extensive proximal DVT.

past_medical_history: Left total knee replacement 3 weeks ago (recent surgery is major risk factor)
surgical_history: Left total knee replacement (3 weeks ago)
medications: Acetaminophen 1000mg three times daily for post-surgical pain
Oxycodone 5mg every 6 hours as needed (taking 2-3 times daily currently)
Aspirin 81mg daily (resumed 1 week post-op per surgeon)
Calcium 600mg with Vitamin D twice daily
allergies: Heparin (history of heparin-induced thrombocytopenia with prior hospitalization - CRITICAL ALLERGY)

social_history.smoking: Smokes about 5 cigarettes a day, has smoked for 35 years (approximately 9 pack-year history)
social_history.alcohol: 1-2 glasses of wine in the evenings
social_history.occupation: Office manager at a dental practice, currently on medical leave recovering from knee surgery
social_history.living_situation: Lives with husband, has been mostly sedentary recovering from surgery, spending most of the day in her recliner
social_history.exercise: None currently due to surgical recovery, was doing physical therapy but missed last two appointments because of the leg pain
social_history.diet: Generally balanced, has been eating more comfort food during recovery

conditions.mother: DVT at age 58, pulmonary embolism at age 65, on blood thinners, alive at 85
conditions.father: Died of lung cancer at age 70, was a heavy smoker

personality: worried but practical, wants to understand the treatment plan and timeline, concerned about how this affects her knee recovery
communication_style: Organized and methodical - will give a clear timeline of events if allowed to tell the story in order. Asks practical questions ('How long will I be on blood thinners? Can I still do physical therapy? Will this delay my knee recovery?'). May get frustrated if she feels she's not getting straight answers.
pain_description_style: Compares it to known experiences ('It's not like the knee surgery pain, it's deeper, more of a throbbing ache'). Good at distinguishing between surgical pain and this new pain. Will accurately point to where it hurts and describe what makes it worse.

critical_findings: Extensive proximal DVT (common femoral to popliteal vein) - high risk for pulmonary embolism
CRITICAL: History of heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (HIT) - CANNOT use heparin or LMWH
Recent surgery (TKR 3 weeks ago) as major DVT risk factor
New mild dyspnea - must evaluate for concurrent pulmonary embolism
Strong family history of VTE (mother with DVT/PE, sister with pregnancy clots, daughter with Factor V Leiden)
Family history suggests possible inherited thrombophilia (Factor V Leiden in daughter)
Active smoker and obese - additional VTE risk factors
Tachycardia (HR 96) and low-grade temperature may indicate clot burden
rubric.diagnosis: Acute Proximal Deep Vein Thrombosis, Left Lower Extremity
recommended_interventions: AVOID ALL HEPARIN PRODUCTS (unfractionated heparin AND LMWH) due to HIT history
Initiate non-heparin anticoagulation: argatroban IV drip or fondaparinux, then transition to DOAC
CT pulmonary angiography given new dyspnea to rule out pulmonary embolism
Compression stockings (after acute phase, not on acutely swollen leg initially)
Hematology consult for HIT history and possible thrombophilia workup
Vascular surgery consult if considering catheter-directed therapy for extensive proximal DVT
Coordinate with orthopedic surgeon regarding knee recovery timeline
Thrombophilia workup after acute treatment (Factor V Leiden, prothrombin gene mutation, antiphospholipid antibodies)
